name: Tajikistan (1953–1992)

colors.symbolism: the unity of the republic and the workers' revolution
cotton production, the basis of Tajik agriculture
the republic's orchards and other agricultural produce
the hammer and sickle of socialism

description: A red field charged with a horizontal white stripe and a narrower green stripe across the centre, with a gold hammer and sickle and a gold-bordered red five-pointed star in the upper hoist. The bands run red (top half), white, green, and red along the bottom.
history: The final flag of the Tajik SSR, designed by M.P. Shlykov and adopted on 20 March 1953 (effective 20 April 1953). The white and green stripes evoke the pan-Iranian colours of the republic's Persian-descended culture. After Tajikistan's independence on 9 September 1991, the flag remained in use as the national flag until a wholly new flag was adopted on 24 November 1992 — the last post-Soviet state to replace its Soviet-era flag.

features.symbolism: red for the workers' revolution, white for cotton, green for agriculture
the union of industrial workers and peasants
the Communist Party leading the proletariat
